title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 20 lines).
summary_text: - High: [`auto-push-after-commit.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh#L27) treats commit success as “stdout does not contain `error|fatal`”, then unconditionally runs `git push` on any tracked branch at [L35](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh#L35). A failed/aborted commit that reports on stderr or via exit code can still publish prior local commits unexpectedly. - Medium: [`eval-skill.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L380) now hard-skips `claude`, `codex`, `cmux`, `gh`, etc. from existence checks instead of verifying them with `command -v`. That regresses the “phantom command” detector into false negatives for exactly the non-portable tools this check is supposed to catch. - Medium: [`freee-client.mjs`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/scripts/freee-client.mjs#L171) adds retries, but only for errors manually tagged `retryable`; real transient failures from `execFileSync` itself (timeout/spawn/MCP startup issues) fall through the outer catch and abort on the first attempt at [L216](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/scripts/freee-client.mjs#L216). The retry loop therefore misses the most likely transient failure mode. - Low: The worktree includes a large amount of generated state (`experience.jsonl`, `.project-init-status`, etc.). If those are intentional, fine; otherwise they will create noisy reviews and accidental commits without adding executable value.
